ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., country, region) */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., country, state, city) */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the location is situated */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_hierarchy" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "parent_location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent location */,
  "level" INTEGER /* Level of the location in the hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sexes" (
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sex */,
  "sex_name" TEXT /* Name of the sex (e.g., Male, Female, Both) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sex_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group_name" TEXT /* Name of the age group (e.g., 0-14 years) */,
  "min_age" INTEGER /* Minimum age in the age group */,
  "max_age" INTEGER /* Maximum age in the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year when the data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measures" (
  "measure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measure */,
  "measure_name" TEXT /* Name of the measure (e.g., Population) */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., Number) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the measure (e.g., people, years) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the data was collected */,
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sex (e.g., Male, Female, Both) */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the age group (e.g., 0-14 years) */,
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Year when the data was collected */,
  "measure_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measure (e.g., Population, Life Expectancy) */,
  "val" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the estimate */,
  "lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the estimate */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., survey, report, research) */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_measure_id FOREIGN KEY ("measure_id") REFERENCES measures ("measure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_year_id FOREIGN KEY ("year_id") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_sex_id FOREIGN KEY ("sex_id") REFERENCES sexes ("sex_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "data_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., survey, report, research) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point that was changed */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the change was made */,
  "old_val" REAL /* Old value of the data point */,
  "new_val" REAL /* New value of the data point */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
